demographics.living_situation: lives alone in a private residence

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My vision in my right eye went blurry a few hours ago, and my cheek feels like it has pins and needles. I thought I was having a stroke.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on out of nowhere this afternoon and it really frightened me — I called because I was scared it might be a stroke and I didn't want to wait.

symptom_narrative.duration: still there now, a few hours
symptom_narrative.character: started as a dull ache behind my right eye, worse when I move the eye, then my central vision in that eye went blurry; at the same time my right cheek started tingling like pins and needles
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the blur is mild-to-moderate and the tingling is annoying rather than painful, but the whole thing scared me a lot
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right eye and the right side of my face / cheek
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on over an hour or two and has stayed fairly constant since
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving my right eye makes the ache behind it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no headache to speak of, no nausea, no flashing lights; I don't feel weak and my speech feels normal to me
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this has ever happened before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can still walk around and do things normally, I'm just frightened and keep checking my vision

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; was at home when it started; feeling very anxious and keeps searching her symptoms online

communication_style.typical_message_style: asks repeatedly for reassurance and voices fear of stroke and of multiple sclerosis
